data.reasoning: Both models failed to analyze PKGBUILD (critical build script) due to incorrect binary file classification. No actual security analysis performed. PKGBUILD is essential for assessing AUR package safety.
coverage_issues: Critical failure: Both models skipped PKGBUILD (core build instructions) due to false binary file detection
No review of .SRCINFO, install scripts, or source files that might be referenced
false_negatives: Potential risks in PKGBUILD (malicious commands, unsafe dependencies)
Undetected install hooks or build-time network access patterns
learnings: Improve binary file detection - PKGBUILDs should never be binary files
Prioritize PKGBUILD analysis first before other files
Add file type verification step to prevent false binary classification
Implement fallback analysis for misclassified critical files
